======= RMA01887Hendrik Edelman papers13-1-1887Compiled by Julia ParkerDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Hendrik Edelman papers
Edelman, Hendrik.
Edelman, Hendrik. (Title of work: Dutch-American bibliography 1693-1794.)
13-1-1887
10 cubic feet. 10 cubic feet.
1969-2007. Subject files Alphbetical files of Assistant Director Hendrik Edelman concerning collection development. Collection material in English 2075360
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Julia ParkerDhaval Mehta
Date completed:
February 2004April 2011
EAD encoding:
Julia ParkerPeter MartinezDhaval Mehta
Date modified:
Dhaval Mehta, April 2011
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Hendrik Edelman served as Assistant Director of the Cornell University Libraries from 1969 to 1979, and then became University Librarian at Rutgers University.
Cite As:
Hendrik Edelman papers, #13-1-1887. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Includes alphabetical files of Assistant Director Hendrik Edelman as the Assistant Director of the Cornell University Libraries concerning collection development, 1969-1974.
Scope and content
Also, files relating to research on his book, Dutch-American Bibliography 1693-1794, a descriptive catalog of Dutch-language books, pamphlets, and almanacs printed in America, 1974; research, consulting, teaching, and other professional papers relating to immigrant presses in America and to library collection development; videos of a Pennsylvania State Library teleconference on collection development; and publications by Edelman.
Librarians -- United States. Dutch imprints -- United States -- Publishing -- History. Libraries and publishing -- United States. Academic libraries -- Collection development. Collection development (Libraries). Cornell University. Library. Assistant Director

Scope and Contents
Includes notes, reprints, copies and "A Guide to the Archives of the Netherlands Museum,Holland, Michigan, 1978"Lectures, research notes, Dutch-American bibliography5
Scope and Contents
Includes notes, reprints, copies and Collection Development Tapes from the Suffolk Community CollegeDutch-American bibliography and note cards, microfilm, 1976 CLR file6Research, consulting, professional, immigrant presses in AmericaProfessional papers71Foreign language research: America notes72Arab73Armenian74Basque75Belorussian76Bulgarian77Carpatho-Rusyns78Chinese79Croats710Czech711Danes712Dutch713Estonians714Finns715Flemish716French717French718German719Greek720Hungarian721Icelandic722Italian723Japanese724Korean725Latvian726Lithuanian727Norwegian728Polish729Portuguese730Rumanian731Russian732Spanish, Cuban - Mexican - Puerto Rican733Swedish734Swiss735Ukrainian736Vietnamese737Welsh738Yiddish - Hebrew739Yugoslav, Serbian - Slovak - Slovenian740Suriname Reports741Professional Correspondence1986-1987742Preliminary Marketing Report for Research Publications Inc743List of materials on The Detschnationale Volkspartei744Pittsburgh use study745The Collections of the Historical Society of Pennsylvania746Reviews in the Library Quarterly747correspondence: NYHS747Report: A Study on Materials Availability, Adelphi University748Discussion on German Periodicals, by Charles Brown749Edelman's Report on Collections750Edelman publications8
Scope and Contents
Includes Logos, Library Journal, the Book Publishing Annual, Christian Book Publishing and Distribution and publications on eighteenth century English books, library research, computing etc. Also includes articles and interviews by EdelmanEdelman and library publications9
Scope and Contents
Includes books on library planning, library search tools, library collection management, university libraries, library journals, computer innovation, obituaries etc.Planning1975101Collection Analysis Project Explanatory Notes102"Selection Methodology in Academic Libraries", paper presented at ALA preconference1977 June103Report on the First Year's Activities Under the Cornell Mellon Project1978 Nov.104General Correspondence105AcquisitionsBangladesh106China107East Asia108Japan109Pakistan1010Singapore/Malaya1011Thailand, SE Asia1012African Acquisitions1970's1013Southeast Asia- Correspondence1960's-1980's1014America's Acquisitions1970's1010Arab & Australia Acquisitions1970's1016Canada Acquisitions1970's1017Austria & Egypt Acquisitions1970's1018European Acquisitions1970's1019France Acquisitions1970's1020Germany Acquisitions1970's1021Italy & Liechtenstein Acquisitions1970's1022Netherlands Acquisitions1970's1023Africana Studies & Res.
